content: Hey, I'm Ellis, nice to chat with you. I've been thinking a lot about environmental concerns lately, especially with the recent floods in Austin, TX, where I used to live.
Hi Ellis, nice to meet you! That's a great topic. The floods in Austin must have been devastating. What made you think about environmental concerns?

Well, I'm a Real Estate Agent in Denver, CO, now, and I see the impact of climate change on the local housing market. I've been trying to reduce my carbon footprint by driving my vintage car less and cooking more Thai food at home, which I love.
That's a great effort, Ellis! Reducing carbon footprint is crucial. What kind of Thai dishes do you like to cook?
I'm a big fan of Pad Thai and Green Curry. I've also been trying to get into urban sketching to document the changes in the cityscape. Maybe I can even sketch some of the flooding in Austin.

That sounds like a great hobby! Documenting the changes in the cityscape can be a powerful way to raise awareness about environmental concerns. Have you thought about showcasing your art on social media or a blog?

Actually, I have a travel blog where I post about my adventures and the environmental issues I encounter. I'm planning a trip to Thailand next year to learn more about sustainable tourism and try some authentic Thai cuisine.
That sounds like an amazing trip! Sustainable tourism is a great way to promote environmental conservation. What inspired you to start your travel blog?

I started it about 5 years ago, when I was 20, and I've been posting regularly ever since. I've got about 1,000 followers now, and it's been a great way to connect with like-minded people and share my passion for travel and environmentalism.

That's impressive, Ellis! Building a community around your blog can be a great way to raise awareness about environmental concerns. What do you think is the most pressing environmental issue facing Denver, CO, right now?

I think it's the air quality. With the nearby oil refineries and the growing population, it's getting harder to breathe in the city. I've been trying to reduce my own impact by using public transport and carpooling with my colleagues, like my friend, Rachel.

Air quality is a significant concern in many cities. It's great that you're taking steps to reduce your impact. Have you considered getting involved in local environmental initiatives or advocacy groups?

Yes, I've been thinking about it. I've got a meeting with a local environmental group on March 15th to discuss potential collaborations. I'm excited to see how I can make a difference in my community.
That's a great step, Ellis! Collaborating with local groups can be a powerful way to drive change. What do you hope to achieve from this meeting?

I hope to learn more about their initiatives and see how I can contribute my skills as a Real Estate Agent to help promote sustainable development in Denver. Maybe I can even help them find a new office space that's more energy-efficient.
That's a great idea, Ellis! Your expertise could be really valuable to their cause. I'm sure you'll make a positive impact.

Thanks, I'm looking forward to it. I'll be sure to post about it on my blog and social media to raise awareness and inspire others to take action. Maybe I can even get my parents, who are currently living with me, involved in the initiative.
That's a great way to spread the word, Ellis. Every bit counts, and inspiring others to take action can have a ripple effect. Good luck with your meeting and your environmental endeavors!

Thanks, I appreciate your support. I'm excited to see what the future holds for Denver and for my own environmental initiatives. I'm planning to start a new project on April 1st to reduce waste in my community.
That sounds like a great project, Ellis! Reducing waste is a crucial step towards a more sustainable future. I'm sure you'll make a positive impact with your new project.

I hope so, I've been thinking about it for a while now. I've got a lot of ideas and I'm excited to get started. Maybe I can even collaborate with some of my colleagues, like my friend, Mike, who's also passionate about environmentalism.
That's a great idea, Ellis! Collaborating with like-minded people can be a powerful way to drive change. I'm sure you'll achieve great things with your new project.
